text:
Most people will still be “learning AI” in 2026.

A small group will be shipping, automating, and replacing workflows with it.

These 9 skills decide which side you’re on 

• Prompt Engineering
(ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini)

• AI Workflow Automation
(Zapier, Make, n8n)

• AI

High-Paying Career Paths of 2026:

Path A:
Learn AI-native smart contracts, AI agents, oracle and LLM integrations, plus security basics. Join a serious AI x Crypto protocol. Earn $70k–$150k plus tokens.

Path B:
Go deep into infrastructure. Validators, nodes, DePIN, ZK, privacy

The only AI/ML concepts you need to know (practical 2026)

• LLM basics ⇒ Tokens, Context window, Temperature / Top-p
• Prompt engineering ⇒ Chain-of-thought, Few-shot, Role prompting
• RAG ⇒ Embeddings, Vector DBs (Pinecone / PGVector), Retrieval + generation
•

Full-Stack AI Engineer 2026: ML, Deep Learning, GenerativeAI


https://
clcoding.com/2026/03/full-s
tack-ai-engineer-2026-ml-deep.html
…

This is all you need to start your AI/ML journey in 2026:

Give this roadmap to ChatGPT and ask for in-depth explanations.

1. Python, statistics/probability, and math (learn together)
2. Understand basics of ML algorithms
3. Deep Learning basics
4. Optimizers and activation

Dear ML folks,

Everyone knows about  
- Claude  
- GPT-5o  
- LangChain  
- n8n  

But what we know will be far more valuable in the coming years. As coding becomes so easy, people's attention spans are shrinking significantly.

Most won't dive into deep learning, transformers,

Apple is spending $600B on AI.
Meta is spending $600B on AI.
OpenAI + Oracle are spending $500B on AI.

That means these jobs will be in demand:

• Cable & electrical work
• Cooling / HVAC
• Hardware setup
• Network support
• Security (physical + cyber)
• Systems admin
•

Top Tech Jobs for 2026

1. Data Scientist
2. Cybersecurity
3. AI/ML Engineer
4. Software Developer (Full Stack)
5. Cloud Engineer / DevOps Engineer
6. UX/UI Designer
7. Product Manager (Tech)
